exemplar.title: How might the context in which knowledge is presented influence whether it is accepted or rejected?

detailedExemplarData.title: How might the context in which knowledge is presented influence whether it is accepted or rejected?

strengths: Clear identification of three specific, real-world objects with contextual detail
Consistent and explicit links made between each object and IA Prompt 24 (narrative knowledge, live immediacy, cultural background)
Dedicated works cited section demonstrating attention to academic conventions
Strong exhibition title that frames the TOK exploration effectively
weaknesses: Justifications occasionally drift into plot summary rather than analytical depth
TOK terminology and concepts (e.g., shared vs. personal knowledge, âtribes of knowersâ) are invoked without precise definition or systematic use
Reliance on anecdotal evidence (IMDb review, single quotations) limits the robustness of supporting arguments
Citations are inconsistent or incomplete (HTML encoding, missing dates, incomplete footnotes)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition meets the descriptors for the 7â8 band: three clearly identified objects with specific real-world contexts, well-explained links to Prompt 24, and a coherent justification of each objectâs contribution to understanding how context shapes knowledge reception. The work demonstrates strong structural organization, clear evidential support in places (e.g., works cited), and effective framing of the TOK question. However, analysis is unevenâthe commentary sometimes slips into descriptive summary, TOK concepts lack precision and consistent definition, and evidence is anecdotal rather than systematically sourced. Citation inconsistencies further detract from academic rigor. These strengths and shortcomings place the submission firmly at the top of the 7â8 band but just below the sustained depth and precision required for a 9â10. Therefore, the final score of 8 accurately reflects the overall quality.

annotations.comment: Suggestion: Introduce and explain the TOK concept of âtribes of knowersâ here, linking it explicitly to definitions in the TOK glossary.
Suggestion: The filmographic citation is clear but lacks a consistent air date format; follow one standardized citation style throughout.
Strength: The exhibition title is clearly presented, immediately establishing the context and focus of the TOK exploration.
Suggestion: Introduce a brief overview in your own words that outlines how you will explore Prompt 24, to guide the reader before presenting the objects.
Suggestion: Specify the broadcast date and target audience demographics in the caption to strengthen the real-world context of this object.
Weakness: This paragraph primarily summarizes the episodeâs plot rather than analyzing how its context influences viewersâ acceptance or rejection of knowledge, as required by the prompt.
Weakness: The term âtribes of knowersâ is invoked but not defined or linked to TOK concepts such as shared versus personal knowledge, which weakens the analytical depth.
Question: Can you clarify and complete the Sam Adams quotation, including the full citation, to ensure the evidence fully supports your justification?
Suggestion: This statement reiterates viewer misunderstandings but would benefit from linking those directly to how prior narrative knowledge shapes acceptance or rejection in TOK terms.
Suggestion: The connection to Shelleyâs poem is implied but remains vague; explicitly discuss how intertextual awareness influences viewersâ creation of meaning.
Strength: This sentence makes a clear link to Prompt 24 by noting prior narrative knowledge, demonstrating an adequate grasp of how context influences interpretation.
Suggestion: The concluding link to context and acceptance is apt, yet lacks precise TOK terminology; consider referencing concepts like perspective or paradigm.
Weakness: This section describes production choices for the live episode but needs clearer justification of how live context alters audience knowledge acceptance, rather than focusing on format.
Suggestion: Incorporate audience reaction data (e.g., viewer surveys or critical essays) to support claims about live vs. prerecorded comprehension and engagement.
Suggestion: Include the date and episode number in the caption to anchor this object more precisely in its real-world context.
Weakness: The footnote citation for the Taskmaster envelope is incomplete and uses HTML encoding; ensure you use a consistent, clear citation style.
Suggestion: Relying on a single IMDb review limits the breadth of perspectives; consider incorporating academic or media critiques to deepen your analysis.
Suggestion: This paragraph asserts variation in viewer reception but could better articulate how live versus prerecorded context affects knowledge framing in TOK terminology.
Question: Can you specify which âknowersâ groups rejected or accepted this episode and explain why? That will clarify your argument.
Suggestion: The image of the Taskmaster envelope is relevant but could be accompanied by a brief description of its visual features to link form and context.
Weakness: The description of Taskmaster NZ provides context, but the link to how the envelopeâs ambiguity influences knowledge acceptance remains underdeveloped.
Suggestion: The example of âFlyâ is useful; consider analyzing how contestantsâ personal and cultural backgrounds led to divergent interpretations, to deepen your link to the prompt.
Weakness: The IMDb review citation is missing the reviewerâs full name and proper URL formatting; ensure all sources are fully and consistently cited.
Suggestion: The BBC Two episode citation is adequate but could benefit from including the broadcast date in a consistent style.
Strength: A dedicated works cited section demonstrates attention to academic conventions, reinforcing the credibility of your analysis.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content:
The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA

these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not

There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
